parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;" value="Note that the post processor only gets executed if there was no internal error. As the output_generator knows no real errors (except the given internal one), it is up to the developer to control whether the post processor is supposed to get triggered or not. For the large majority of use cases, you do NOT need post processing - therefore, don't pass along a post processing function to your class' instance unless you really need that functionality" div.div:
This code gets only executed in those cases
where an APRS message passes the APRS
filter criteria AND is detected as 'no duplicate'
The following diagram is a simplified view of the core-aprs-client's processing flow.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 270px; height: 1px; padding-top: 216px; margin-left: 433px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Check if msg is duplicate and ignore message if necessary

box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 318px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1095px; margin-left: 26px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Note that the post processor only gets executed if there was no internal error. As the output_generator knows no real errors (except the given internal one), it is up to the developer to control whether the post processor is supposed to get triggered or not. For the large majority of use cases, you do NOT need post processing - therefore, don't pass along a post processing function to your class' instance unless you really need that functionality
